title: How to Get Into
subtitle: A methodical, reverse-engineered and brutally honest guide to the application and the interview
author: Antoine Carossio
authorTagline: Co-founder & CTO of Escape · YC W23
authorCredentials: Forbes 30 Under 30 · École Polytechnique × HEC Paris · ex-Apple AI Researcher · RSA / Black Hat / DEF CON speaker
authorBio: Antoine Carossio is the co-founder and CTO of Escape, the AI-native offensive security platform he took from zero to 100+ enterprise customers and an $18M Series A led by Balderton Capital. He went through Y Combinator in the W23 batch. Before Escape he was an AI researcher at Apple and a penetration tester for the French Ministry for the Armed Forces, and he holds a double master's from École Polytechnique and HEC Paris. He speaks on AI and cybersecurity at RSA, Black Hat, and DEF CON, advises Tier-1 VC funds on technical due diligence, and was named to the Forbes 30 Under 30. Since his own batch he has mentored a number of startups that went on to be admitted to YC.

cta.title: Want a second pair of eyes?
cta.text: Follow me on LinkedIn and send me a message. I give founders direct mentoring on their YC application — and write recommendations for the ones I work closely with.

coverByline: by the co-founder & CTO of Escape (YC W23 · $18M Series A)
backTagline: YC selection is a process you can reverse-engineer and prepare for.
backBlurb: Most applications are rejected for the same reasons. This guide breaks the written application and the 10-minute interview down into a single repeatable framework — product-market fit reasoning — and shows, concretely, how to answer any question the right way. It is written by a founder who went through W23 and has since coached startups into the batch.
backBullets: The PMF framework: the five pillars behind every answer
Why specificity is the universal tell — and how to add it
How to prepare for the interview without over-rehearsing
Recommendations, momentum, and applying every batch
escapeBlurb: Escape is the AI-native offensive security platform. Its agents continuously discover, test, and fix vulnerabilities directly inside engineering workflows — attack-surface management, business-logic-aware DAST, and continuous AI pentesting. Founded in 2020 and a member of the YC W23 batch, Escape raised an $18M Series A led by Balderton Capital (with Uncorrelated Ventures, IRIS, and Y Combinator) and is trusted by 2,000+ security teams worldwide.
